basic_details.job_title: Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 56 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Office of the Principal District and Sessions Judge, Shivamogga, Karnataka
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Shortlisting based on SSLC/qualifying exam marks (1:10 ratio) followed by interview (10 marks) on Kannada reading/writing ability and general knowledge
basic_details.short_information: The Office of the Principal District and Sessions Judge, Shivamogga, Karnataka has released a notification inviting online applications for direct recruitment to 56 vacant posts of Peon (ಜವಾನ) in the Shivamogga District Court unit. The recruitment follows directions from the Karnataka High Court and applicable State Government orders governing reservation and recruitment rules.

application_fee.details: General category candidates: ₹200/- Category 2A, 2B, 3A, and 3B candidates: ₹100/- SC, ST, Category-1, and disabled candidates: Exempted from application fee Fee must be paid only online via State Bank Collect (SBI e-pay / net banking / credit card / debit card / UPI), through the link on the court website; demand drafts, postal orders, money orders, and challans are not accepted Applications with unpaid fee will be rejected; fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances

age_limit.details: Minimum age: 18 years Maximum age – General category: 35 + 5* years Maximum age – Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B: 38 + 5* years Maximum age – SC, ST, Category-1: 40 + 5* years *A one-time additional relaxation of 5 years in the maximum age limit is granted to candidates of all categories participating in the selection process, as per Government Order dated 29.01.2026 Further age relaxations apply for government employees/ex-employees of State Government, local authorities, or government corporations (up to years of service or 10 years, whichever is less), ex-servicemen (service years + 3 years), NCC whole-time instructors, Gram Sevaks, persons with disabilities (10 years), Census Department employees (service years or 5 years, whichever is less), widows (10 years), and released bonded labourers (10 years), as per the Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977

qualification.details: Candidates who do not possess the required qualification but participate in the recruitment process will have their applications rejected and will not be considered for recruitment.
qualification.education_qualification: Pass in SSLC (10th) conducted by the Karnataka Secondary Education Examination Board, or an equivalent examination (CBSE/ICSE Class 10, other State Board Class 10, NIOS Secondary level course, or KSOU Secondary level course, as clarified vide Government Circular dated 27.02.2018)

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be paid in the pay scale of ₹27,000-650-29,600-725-32,500-800-35,700-900-39,300-1,000-43,300-1,125-46,675, along with other applicable allowances.

selection_process: Selection will be based on the Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules
1977 and the Karnataka Subordinate Courts (Clerical and Other Posts) (Recruitment) Rules
1982 (as amended in 2007 and 2021). Based on the overall percentage of marks obtained in the qualifying examination (SSLC)
candidates will be shortlisted at a ratio of 1:10 (10 candidates per vacancy) with the highest marks
and called for interview. The interview will assess the candidate's ability to read and write Kannada

and carries 10 marks. Final selection will be based on marks scored in the interview. Candidates selected will undergo a probation period of 2 years as per the Karnataka Civil Services (Probation) Rules
extendable if necessary. Pension benefits will apply as per applicable Government Orders (No. FD(SPL)/04 P/E/T/2025 dated 31.03.2006 and No. AI 34 PES 2018 dated 23.06.2018
and related orders). The recruitment order issued under this notification will be subject to the final orders of the Karnataka High Court in W.P. No. 200448/2025
how_to_apply: Ensure you fulfil the eligibility criteria for age, qualification, and language before applying.
Visit the Shivamogga District Court website: https://shivamogga.dcourts.gov.in/notice-category/recruitments/ and access the recruitment link.
Read all instructions carefully before filling the online application.
Submit only one application, mentioning all claimed reservations within that single application.
Mandatorily enter your mobile number, and email ID if available.
Upload your scanned photograph and signature as per the specified size and format guidelines.
Upload a clear scanned copy of your SSLC (or equivalent) marksheet in PDF format.
Complete all requested details in the online application and proceed to pay the prescribed fee via SBI e-pay (net banking/credit card/debit card/UPI).
Download and retain the submitted application and fee payment receipt until the recruitment process is complete; produce it at the time of interview.
Apply well in advance of the last date (24 August 2026, 11:59 pm) to avoid last-minute issues.
important_instructions: Applications submitted through any mode other than online will not be considered. Incomplete applications or those with incorrect information will be rejected. Candidates already employed in any State/Central Government department or public sector undertaking must indicate this in the application, and must produce a No Objection Certificate from their department as per Rule 11 of the Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977, if selected. Candidates claiming reservation benefits must possess the relevant certificates issued by competent authorities on or before the last date of application. If SSLC marks are recorded in grades or CGPA, candidates must obtain a certificate converting these to marks from the competent authority before the last date, and enter maximum marks, marks obtained, and overall percentage in the application. At the time of document verification/interview, candidates must produce original certificates for educational qualification, date of birth, caste, and any claimed reservation; candidature will be rejected and criminal prosecution may follow if information is found false. Candidates found using unfair or improper means during the recruitment process will not be considered for recruitment. Eligible candidates will be informed via SMS and email; the list of candidates eligible for interview will be published on the court website, and interview calls will be based on this list. The authority will not be responsible if SMS or email communication does not reach a candidate.
Candidates interested in similar opportunities can also explore more Karnataka District Court Recruitment, Peon/Group D Jobs, and Karnataka Government Jobs currently open for application.

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026 Notification Out
content_sections.text: The notification (No. ADM.DJS.16342/2026, dated 20 July 2026) has been issued by the Principal District and Sessions Judge, Shivamogga, for filling 56 vacant Peon posts across various courts under the Shivamogga District and Sessions Court unit through direct recruitment. Notification: Download Here Apply Online / Official Website: Apply Here

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026 - Quick Overview

rows.details: Office of the Principal District and Sessions Judge, Shivamogga, Karnataka
Various courts under Shivamogga District Court unit, Karnataka
Shortlisting based on SSLC/qualifying exam marks (1:10 ratio) followed by interview (10 marks) on Kannada reading/writing ability and general knowledge

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

rows.event: Online Application & Fee Payment End Date

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Vacancy 2026 Details
content_sections.text: A total of 56 vacant posts of Peon have been notified, category-wise, as follows: Note: One post under "SC Category-C" is reserved for the 59 notified backward castes; if no suitable candidate is available from these castes, the post will be given to other castes within Category-C. Horizontal reservations for women, rural candidates, persons with disabilities, ex-servicemen, Kannada-medium candidates, project-displaced persons, and transgender candidates apply within the above vertical categories as per the notification. If suitable candidates are not available under disability, women, project-displaced, transgender, or ex-servicemen reservation, such vacancies will be carried forward to future recruitment or filled by other eligible candidates in the same category.

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates who do not possess the required qualification but participate in the recruitment process will have their applications rejected and will not be considered for recruitment.

rows.details: Pass in SSLC (10th) conducted by the Karnataka Secondary Education Examination Board, or an equivalent examination (CBSE/ICSE Class 10, other State Board Class 10, NIOS Secondary level course, or KSOU Secondary level course, as clarified vide Government Circular dated 27.02.2018)
Must be able to read and write Kannada

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Age Limit 2026
content_sections.text: Minimum age: 18 years Maximum age – General category: 35 + 5* years Maximum age – Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B: 38 + 5* years Maximum age – SC, ST, Category-1: 40 + 5* years *A one-time additional relaxation of 5 years in the maximum age limit is granted to candidates of all categories participating in the selection process, as per Government Order dated 29.01.2026 Further age relaxations apply for government employees/ex-employees of State Government, local authorities, or government corporations (up to years of service or 10 years, whichever is less), ex-servicemen (service years + 3 years), NCC whole-time instructors, Gram Sevaks, persons with disabilities (10 years), Census Department employees (service years or 5 years, whichever is less), widows (10 years), and released bonded labourers (10 years), as per the Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Salary Structure 2026
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be paid in the pay scale of ₹27,000-650-29,600-725-32,500-800-35,700-900-39,300-1,000-43,300-1,125-46,675, along with other applicable allowances.

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: General category candidates: ₹200/- Category 2A, 2B, 3A, and 3B candidates: ₹100/- SC, ST, Category-1, and disabled candidates: Exempted from application fee Fee must be paid only online via State Bank Collect (SBI e-pay / net banking / credit card / debit card / UPI), through the link on the court website; demand drafts, postal orders, money orders, and challans are not accepted Applications with unpaid fee will be rejected; fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026 Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection will be based on the Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977 and the Karnataka Subordinate Courts (Clerical and Other Posts) (Recruitment) Rules, 1982 (as amended in 2007 and 2021). Based on the overall percentage of marks obtained in the qualifying examination (SSLC), candidates will be shortlisted at a ratio of 1:10 (10 candidates per vacancy) with the highest marks, and called for interview. The interview will assess the candidate's ability to read and write Kannada, and general knowledge, and carries 10 marks. Final selection will be based on marks scored in the interview. Candidates selected will undergo a probation period of 2 years as per the Karnataka Civil Services (Probation) Rules, 1977, extendable if necessary. Pension benefits will apply as per applicable Government Orders (No. FD(SPL)/04 P/E/T/2025 dated 31.03.2006 and No. AI 34 PES 2018 dated 23.06.2018, and related orders). The recruitment order issued under this notification will be subject to the final orders of the Karnataka High Court in W.P. No. 200448/2025, 17968/2025, and 3145/2023.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for Shivamogga District Court Peon Application
content_sections.text: Scanned copy of SSLC (or equivalent) marksheet, uploaded clearly in PDF format Recent color passport-size photograph (5 cm x 3.6 cm, max 50 KB, .jpg format, white background; signature must not be made over the photograph) Signature in black ballpoint pen on white paper, scanned (2.5 cm x 7.5 cm, max 26 KB, .jpg format) Caste/category certificate in the prescribed format from a competent authority, for candidates claiming SC/ST/Category-1/2A/2B/3A/3B reservation Certificates from competent authorities for candidates claiming reservation under rural, disability, Kannada-medium, project-displaced, ex-servicemen, or transgender categories Creamy Layer certificate, for general-merit candidates claiming rural category reservation Certificates for age relaxation, where claimed (disability, ex-servicemen, widow status, etc.) No Objection Certificate from current employer, for candidates already employed in State/Central Government or PSUs, if selected

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Ensure you fulfil the eligibility criteria for age, qualification, and language before applying. Visit the Shivamogga District Court website: https://shivamogga.dcourts.gov.in/notice-category/recruitments/ and access the recruitment link. Read all instructions carefully before filling the online application. Submit only one application, mentioning all claimed reservations within that single application. Mandatorily enter your mobile number, and email ID if available. Upload your scanned photograph and signature as per the specified size and format guidelines. Upload a clear scanned copy of your SSLC (or equivalent) marksheet in PDF format. Complete all requested details in the online application and proceed to pay the prescribed fee via SBI e-pay (net banking/credit card/debit card/UPI). Download and retain the submitted application and fee payment receipt until the recruitment process is complete; produce it at the time of interview. Apply well in advance of the last date (24 August 2026, 11:59 pm) to avoid last-minute issues.

content_sections.heading: Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026 Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Applications submitted through any mode other than online will not be considered. Incomplete applications or those with incorrect information will be rejected. Candidates already employed in any State/Central Government department or public sector undertaking must indicate this in the application, and must produce a No Objection Certificate from their department as per Rule 11 of the Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977, if selected. Candidates claiming reservation benefits must possess the relevant certificates issued by competent authorities on or before the last date of application. If SSLC marks are recorded in grades or CGPA, candidates must obtain a certificate converting these to marks from the competent authority before the last date, and enter maximum marks, marks obtained, and overall percentage in the application. At the time of document verification/interview, candidates must produce original certificates for educational qualification, date of birth, caste, and any claimed reservation; candidature will be rejected and criminal prosecution may follow if information is found false. Candidates found using unfair or improper means during the recruitment process will not be considered for recruitment. Eligible candidates will be informed via SMS and email; the list of candidates eligible for interview will be published on the court website, and interview calls will be based on this list. The authority will not be responsible if SMS or email communication does not reach a candidate. Candidates interested in similar opportunities can also explore more Karnataka District Court Recruitment, Peon/Group D Jobs, and Karnataka Government Jobs currently open for application.

faq.question: Q1. How many Peon vacancies has the Shivamogga District Court announced for 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 56 vacant posts of Peon have been notified across various courts under the Shivamogga District and Sessions Court unit.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Online applications along with fee payment must be submitted by 24 August 2026, till 11:59 pm. Applications open from 23 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the minimum educational qualification required?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed SSLC (10th) conducted by the Karnataka Secondary Education Examination Board, or an equivalent recognized examination, and must be able to read and write Kannada.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Minimum age is 18 years. Maximum age is 35+5 years for General category, 38+5 years for Category 2A/2B/3A/3B, and 40+5 years for SC/ST/Category-1, with further relaxations for specific categories as per rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: ₹200/- for General category, ₹100/- for Category 2A/2B/3A/3B candidates; SC, ST, Category-1, and disabled candidates are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q6. How will candidates be selected for the Peon posts?
faq.answer: Candidates are shortlisted at a 1:10 ratio based on SSLC/qualifying exam marks, followed by an interview (10 marks) assessing Kannada reading/writing ability and general knowledge; final selection is based on interview marks.

faq.question: Q7. How can candidates apply for Shivamogga District Court Peon Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the link provided on the Shivamogga District Court website; no offline or postal applications are accepted.

faq.question: Q8. Is the application fee refundable?
faq.answer: No, once the fee is paid online through SBI e-pay, it is non-refundable under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q9. What documents are required at the time of interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must produce original certificates for educational qualification, date of birth, caste certificate, and any claimed reservation certificates for verification.

faq.question: Q10. Is this recruitment linked to any ongoing court proceedings?
faq.answer: Yes, the recruitment order issued under this notification is subject to the final orders of the Karnataka High Court in W.P. No. 200448/2025, 17968/2025, and 3145/2023.
